Empirical estimates from macro and microeconomic studies cluster around 0.5, though estimates vary considerably (roughly 0.2-1.5 depending on methodology). Campbell and Mankiw (1989) on permanent income hypothesis tests
Lustig and Van Nieuwerburgh (2008) on consumption inequality and IES estimation

Annual calibrations typically use values in the 0.4-0.6 range based on long-run consumption growth responses to interest rate changes.
